what_it_does: Introduces a Design DNA session to a visual factory session, explains the identity assets it has built, frames them as intended input to the factory, and asks for the factory's schema or a composition example.
observations: Written as a direct handoff letter from a Claude Code session working with Tim.
Defines Design DNA as a token-based visual and verbal identity layer, distinct from product, content, or messaging.
Lists concrete assets: stable tokens, warm-cool dial, mockups/probes, template patterns, invariants, voice spec, and AI generation prompts.
States an architecture from primitives through registries, templates, scales, dials, voice, skills, and context.
Says the next step is compiling a portable dist pack for a parametric engine and AI to consume as task-specific slices.
Includes absolute WSL and Windows paths for the repo root and handoff files.
Asks whether the factory has an existing schema and requests one example composition representation.
standouts: Positions identity data as an ingestible substrate for a generation factory.
Emphasizes consumer-defined contract rather than imposing a format.
Connects tokens, templates, invariants, voice, and provenance into one described circuit.
conventions: Markdown letter format with a title, sections, blockquote, bullets, and a signoff.
Uses conversational first-person voice while providing concrete technical inventory.
Uses bold emphasis for key concepts and asks.
concerns: Absolute machine-specific paths may limit portability of the letter itself.
Actionable integration details are deferred to a companion file rather than included here.
Several described assets are asserted without inline examples in this file.
notes: Briefing note: this file is a handoff letter explaining that a tokenized Design DNA pack should feed a visual factory and requesting the factory's ingest contract.
questions: What schema, if any, does the visual factory already use?
What does one factory composition representation look like?
Which parts of the described DNA pack are expected to be runtime inputs versus static compiled assets?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it introduces the Design DNA work, explains why it matters to the visual factory, and asks for schema/composition input.
intention_signals: It seems intended to align two parallel workstreams by presenting the Design DNA output as a structured identity substrate that the visual factory can ingest without forcing a specific format.
novelty: The file treats brand identity as a machine-readable language spanning visual tokens, templates, dials, invariants, voice, and AI prompts rather than as a conventional static style guide.
contribution: Contributes a neutral integration brief for a fused system: a described identity substrate, its intended consumable parts, its invariant constraints, and the open contract questions needed for a generation factory to ingest it.
summary_for_embedding: A Markdown handoff letter introducing the Design DNA pack as a tokenized visual and verbal identity substrate intended to plug into a visual factory, with assets listed and schema/composition inputs requested.
intention_for_embedding: This was for initiating coordination between the Design DNA workstream and the visual factory workstream so the identity pack can be shaped to the factory's ingestion model.
